theme_strategy.rationale: Industrial environments often have varying lighting. Dark mode reduces eye strain in low-light workshops; Light mode is better for office admin and printing.

design_principles: Fusion over Imitation: Industrial ruggedness meets Swiss precision.
Emotion First: The UI should feel 'Reliable', 'Heavy-Duty', and 'Precise'.
Content is King: Data density for repairs and parts is crucial. No wasted whitespace in data tables.
Zoom In: Use bold, large typography for status and critical alerts.
Negative Space: Use it to separate the sidebar and major sections, but keep data dense.
Contrast: High contrast for safety and legibility. Alarms must pop.

headings.usage: Page titles, Card headers, KPI values
headings.rationale: Compact, strong, industrial feel. Reminiscent of serial numbers and machinery labels.

body.rationale: Highly legible, modern, geometric but friendly. excellent for UI numbers.

mono.usage: IDs, Serial Numbers, IP addresses, Logs

primary.name: Industrial Blue
primary.usage: Primary actions, active states, focus rings

secondary.name: Safety Orange
secondary.usage: Warnings, attention-grabbing elements, 'Repair in Progress' status

sidebar.behavior: Fixed left, collapsible on mobile (though desktop-first)

components_strategy.customization: Industrial styling - sharp corners or minimal radius (rounded-md), dense padding.
components_strategy.cards: Flat, 1px border, subtle shadow. Header separated by border.
components_strategy.tables: Dense, distinct headers, row hover effects. Status badges.
components_strategy.buttons: Semi-rounded (rounded-md). Primary: Solid Blue. Secondary: Outline.
components_strategy.inputs: High contrast borders. Clearly defined focus states.

visual_enhancers.textures: Subtle noise texture on dark mode backgrounds to prevent banding and add 'material' feel.
visual_enhancers.borders: Micro-borders. Use 'divide-y' and 'divide-x' for data separation.
visual_enhancers.shadows: crisp, low blur shadows for active elements (pop-overs).

motion.hover: Subtle color shift, no massive scaling.
motion.interactions: Immediate feedback on clicks. Skeleton loaders for data fetching.

dashboard.layout: Grid with KPI Cards (Top), Recent Repairs (Left Main), Alarms (Right Sidebar/Panel)
dashboard.features: Quick Actions (New Repair, New Client) visible immediately.

repairs_list.features: Search bar, Status Filter (Tabs), Date Range Picker, PDF Export Button.

repair_detail.features: Timeline of status changes, Print Button.

alarms.style: Notification Center or Dedicated Page. Priority indicators (Color coded dots).

login_bg.description: Tools on a workbench, focus on precision

instructions_to_main_agent: Setup Tailwind CSS: Create 'tailwind.config.js' and 'postcss.config.js'. Configure content paths to include all JS/JSX files.
Create a 'lib/utils.js' file for the 'cn' utility function (Shadcn standard).
Create a Layout wrapper component that includes the Sidebar and handles the Theme Toggle.
Wrap the App contents in a ThemeProvider (create 'components/theme-provider.js').
Refactor 'Reparacoes.js' (List) to use a modern Table component (create 'components/ui/table.js' first or use a simple Tailwind table structure).
Enhance 'Alarmes' by creating a 'components/AlarmBadge.js' and 'components/AlarmList.js' with priority colors.
Keep all API calls and logic from existing files. Only change the JSX/CSS.
Ensure 'electron.js' window settings allow for a modern frameless look if possible, or at least a clean title bar.
Use 'Barlow Condensed' for all headings. Import it from Google Fonts in 'index.css'.
DO NOT DELETE existing logic/functions. Refactor component by component.
universal_guidelines: Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts. Use Bento grids.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters.
